entities.name: brahman
entities.cite: Time enters as a sakti of Brahman, not as an independent container

entities.name: kālaśakti
entities.cite: Time enters as a sakti of Brahman, not as an independent container

entities.name: sad-bhava-vikāra
entities.cite: the six become yoni, wombs, the generative sources of differentiated being

entities.name: bhāva-bheda
entities.cite: the generative sources of differentiated being

axioms.name: asraya
axioms.cite: Time enters as a sakti of Brahman, not as an independent container

axioms.name: vivartate
axioms.cite: Sequence is thus a feature of how the one appears, not of what it is, which is what the vivarta of 1.1 requires

axioms.name: yoni
axioms.cite: the six become yoni, wombs, the generative sources of differentiated being

axioms.name: adhyahita
axioms.cite: Time in itself is undivided; the partition into moments is laid onto it, not found in it

denials.name: independent_container
denials.cite: Time enters as a sakti of Brahman, not as an independent container

denials.name: intrinsically_divided
denials.cite: Time in itself is undivided; the partition into moments is laid onto it, not found in it

claims.name: vivartate
claims.cite: arise the six modifications beginning with birth

claims.name: yoni
claims.cite: these are the wombs of the differentiation of beings

rejected_readings.label: time_as_independent_absolute
rejected_readings.rendering: Time is an independent absolute power that directly generates the six modifications.
rejected_readings.why: Treats time as an independent absolute rather than a power (sakti) of Brahman, contradicting the commentary's explicit teaching that time enters as a sakti of Brahman, not as an independent container.

rejected_readings.label: intrinsic_temporal_moments
rejected_readings.rendering: Time itself is inherently divided into moments, and these real moments generate the six modifications.
rejected_readings.why: Treats time's divisions as intrinsic to its nature rather than superimposed upon it, reversing the commentary's core teaching about adhyahita-kala (divisions laid onto undivided time, not found in it).

rejected_readings.label: brahman_direct_transformation
rejected_readings.rendering: Brahman transforms into the six modifications of being, which then become differentiated beings.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts that Brahman undergoes real transformation (parinamate) into the six, bypassing the mediation of kalasakti and replacing vivarta (appearance without modification) with parinama (real change). This violates the structure established by the verse's explicit dependence on the power of Time.

rejected_readings.label: modifications_equal_beings
rejected_readings.rendering: The six modifications ARE the differentiated beings themselves, not their sources.
rejected_readings.why: Conflates the yoni (generative sources/wombs) with what is generated from them, eliminating the distinction the commentary carefully preserves between the six modifications (as generative sources) and the differentiated beings that issue from them.

notes: Verse 1.3 extends the vivarta doctrine of 1.1 by making Time (as power of Brahman) the explicit medium of differentiation. The six modifications (birth, existence, alteration, growth, decline, perishes from Nirukta) become yoni—not merely changes in a substance, but generative sources of differentiated being. The key formalization is that these are all manifestations (vivarta) that depend on an undivided power whose divisions are superimposed, not intrinsic—maintaining the non-duality while accounting for multiplicity.
